events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: PPE selection for arc-flash work. The observed field condition is "arc-flash rating below task category (label vs. clothing - DOC+ID)". This means the staged PPE has an arc-flash rating that is below the required category for the task. This is non-compliant and dangerous. Decision should be fail. Risk is critical or high. Work stage is staged. Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete: 0 (staged, work not begun). Value: 0. Confidence high. Findings: arc-flash PPE rating below required task category. Actions: replace with appropriate arc-rated PPE matching or exceeding the incident energy/arc-flash boundary for the task per NFPA 70E. Rationale: NFPA 70E requires arc-rated clothing and PPE matching the incident energy analysis or arc-flash PPE category for the task; OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires appropriate PPE. References: NFPA 70E, OSHA 1910.137 / 1910 Subpart I.

"Staged arc-flash PPE rating is below the task category shown on the equipment label/DOC+ID.",
"Selected clothing does not meet the required arc thermal performance value (ATPV) or PPE category for the energized task."
],
"actions": [
"Halt energized work until correct arc-rated PPE is selected.",
"Verify the arc-flash boundary and incident energy or PPE category from the label/DOC+ID and OEM service data.",
"Replace staged clothing/PPE with arc-rated gear meeting or exceeding the required category (e.g., ATPV/Ebt or Category 1-4 per NFPA 70E).",
"Re-inspect all staged PPE for rating, condition, and fit before work begins."
],
"rationale": "NFPA 70E requires arc-rated clothing and PPE selected to match the incident energy analysis or arc-flash PPE category for the specific task. Using PPE rated below the task category leaves the worker under-protected and is non-compliant. OSHA 1910 Subpart I requires employer-provided PPE appropriate to the identified hazards.",
"references": [
"NFPA 70E (Arc-Flash PPE selection / incident energy analysis)",
"OSHA 1910 Subpart I (PPE general requirements)",
"OSHA 1910.137 (electrical protective equipment)"
]
}
